mnemonics.title: ACID Rain Protects Data
mnemonics.content: ACID: Atomicity (All operations succeed or none), Consistency (Valid state before/after), Isolation (Transactions don't interfere), Durability (Changes survive crashes)

mnemonics.title: BECC a Transaction Pro
mnemonics.content: Transaction Steps: Begin (setAutoCommit(false)), Execute SQL, Check for errors, Choose Commit/Rollback

mnemonics.title: SAVE Your Progress
mnemonics.content: Savepoint Usage: Set savepoint, Add operations, Validate results, Execute commit/rollback to savepoint

mnemonics.title: ACTIVE Transaction States
mnemonics.content: States: Active, Committed, Terminated, In-Failure, View Changes, Ended (Covers life cycle stages)

acronyms.usage: Core transaction properties to ensure reliability

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a grenade explosion - either all pins (operations) are pulled or none detonate
Picture multiple traffic lanes (transactions) with barriers preventing collisions
Think of bookmarks in a novel - you can return to marked positions
Visualize data being carved into stone tablets surviving disasters
Imagine orchestra conductor coordinating multiple musicians (transactions)

analogies.analogy: Like a wedding ceremony - all vows (operations) must complete successfully, or the marriage isn't valid
Similar to airplane seat reservations - once locked by a transaction (passenger), others can't access it
Like a library book checkout system - first user (transaction) gets exclusive access until return (commit)
Assembly line process - raw materials (operations) enter, get processed, and either become final product (commit) or get scrapped (rollback)
